title: Cedar Fence Picket Sandbox
summary: A no-frills octagonal-ish sandbox built entirely from cedar fence pickets over three 2x4 spines — the whole box for the price of a bundle of pickets.
description:
This is the sandbox stripped down to its cheapest honest form: three 2x4 spines (two at the ends, one down the center for stiffness) with cedar fence pickets screwed across the top and down both sides, in that order. There's no frame joinery beyond butted screws, and no cutting at all if your pickets aren't dog-eared — buy them square-topped and the whole build skips a step.

The center 2x4 does real work despite being invisible once the box is finished: without it, a sandbox this size flexes noticeably underfoot and the picket seams start to gap. Orient it the same way as the corner 2x4s (narrow face to the sides) so the screw pattern stays consistent across all three spines.

Build the top and bottom picket runs first, screwed to the 2x4 spines while everything is still lying flat and easy to access, THEN close in the two open ends last — trying to work the end pickets on first makes reaching the interior fasteners much harder. A light final sanding of every top edge matters more here than on most builds: this is a surface small hands and bare feet interact with directly, all summer.

tools.note: Only needed if your pickets are dog-eared and need squaring.

materials.name: Cedar fence picket, 5-1/2" x 6 ft, not dog-eared

cutList.note: No cutting needed if your pickets aren't dog-eared. If dog-eared, trim all pickets to this same length.

steps.title: Build the two end spine pairs
steps.body: Lay two 2x4 spines down on a flat, level surface. Attach two cedar fence pickets to the top with 1-1/4" screws. Build two of these identical assemblies.
materials: Cedar fence picket, 5-1/2" x 6 ft, not dog-eared

steps.title: Join the two ends into a box
steps.body: Attach two cedar pickets to each side of the two spine assemblies, connecting them into the overall sandbox shape.
materials: Cedar fence picket, 5-1/2" x 6 ft, not dog-eared

steps.title: Add the center spine
steps.body: Find the center of the side pickets you just attached and screw the center 2x4 spine in place, with the narrower face oriented toward the sides — the same orientation as the two corner spines. This is what keeps a sandbox this size from flexing underfoot.

steps.title: Top the three spines
steps.body: Place the remaining top pickets along all three spines and attach.
materials: Cedar fence picket, 5-1/2" x 6 ft, not dog-eared

steps.title: Close the ends and finish
steps.body: Screw the remaining pickets to the open ends, flat against the sides. Sand every top edge thoroughly to prevent splinters — this surface gets bare hands and feet all summer. Finish with an exterior product if desired.
materials: Cedar fence picket, 5-1/2" x 6 ft, not dog-eared
